relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus sits beside the sea to teach.
The crowd gathers while he teaches from the boat.
He speaks kingdom mystery through seed and soil.
Some seed is lost on the path.
Some springs quickly without depth.
Without root, heat withers it.
Some is choked by thorns.
Good soil receives and multiplies fruit.
Hearing must become true hearing.
The disciples ask why he speaks in parables.
Mysteries of heaven’s reign are given, not seized.
Receptivity increases capacity.
Parables reveal and conceal according to readiness.
Isaiah’s word describes seeing without perceiving.
A thickened heart resists turning and healing.
Eyes that see and ears that hear are blessed.
Prophets longed to see and hear this moment.
Jesus opens the sower parable.
Without understanding, the word is easily seized from the heart.
Joy without root cannot endure.
Pressure reveals whether the word has depth.
Age-worry and wealth-deception choke fruitfulness.
Understanding bears multiplied fruit.
Good seed is sown in the field.
An enemy sows weeds while people sleep.
Wheat and weeds appear together.
The servants question the weeds.
The householder names enemy work.
Premature uprooting would harm the wheat.
Both are allowed to grow until harvest.
The mustard seed shows smallness becoming shelter.
A tiny seed becomes a tree for birds.
Leaven hidden in flour transforms the whole.
Jesus teaches the crowds in parables.
Hidden things are uttered from the foundation.
In the house, disciples seek explanation.
The Son of Humanity sows the good seed.
The field is the world and the seed belongs to the reign.
Harvest is the completion of the age.
The parable points toward final sorting.
Stumbling-blocks and lawlessness are gathered out.
The warning imagery burns with seriousness.
The righteous shine like the sun in the Father’s reign.
The reign is hidden treasure worth everything.
The reign is a pearl sought by a merchant.
True value reorders all possessions.
The reign is a net gathering every kind from the sea.
Fullness leads to sorting.
At the age’s completion, separation is unveiled.
The warning is repeated with fire and grief imagery.
The disciples say they understand.
A scribe discipled to the reign brings out old and new treasure.
The parables conclude.
In his homeland, familiar eyes stumble over unexpected wisdom.
They reduce him to known family categories.
Familiarity blocks wonder.
A prophet is dishonored in his own place.
Lack of trust limits what can be received there.
